name: Offline Block Coding (Scratch-compatible)
description: Self-hosted block-based coding environment for ages 8+ using the MIT-licensed scratch-gui source code. Independent deployment — not affiliated with or endorsed by the Scratch Foundation. Built from source on first install and served locally; no Scratch.mit.edu dependency, no telemetry. Complements Maker Lab for older learners stepping up from Blockly.
author: Crow

env_vars.name: SCRATCH_HTTP_PORT
env_vars.description: Host port to expose Scratch on. Default 8087.

env_vars.name: SCRATCH_GUI_REF
env_vars.description: scratch-gui git ref (tag, branch, or commit SHA) to build. Defaults to 'master'. Bump with care and retest — upstream doesn't maintain stable tags.

notes: Independent self-hosted deployment of the open-source scratch-gui code (MIT license) — not an official Scratch Foundation product, and the 'Scratch' name/logo are trademarks of the Scratch Foundation. First install builds scratch-gui from source (requires ~2 GB disk + ~5-15 min on a Pi 4 / ~2 min on x86). Runtime footprint after build is ~50 MB static HTML/JS served by nginx. No sign-in, no cloud save — projects download as .sb3 files only. Gated to age 8+ in the Maker Lab recommender; younger learners stay on Blockly.
